response.label: Imports of goods, adjusted for non response, SEK thousand by commodity group according to CN, observations and month
note: From 2025 onwards, a new threshold applies for companies being obliged to report data to Intrastat (intra-EU trade). The threshold is raised for dispatches from SEK 4.5 million to SEK 12.0 million but remains unchanged for arrival (SEK 15.0 million). This leads to more companies falling below the dispatches threshold and fewer companies being obliged to report information on intra-EU dispatches to Statistics Sweden. A higher proportion of the data therefore needs to be estimated and the uncertainty of the detailed data increases. The export data published for 2025 will be based on less reported information than previous years and the tables showing export data adjusted for non-response will rely more on estimates than data from previous years.

Data adjusted for non-response 
 
Companies that import and export below a certain threshold within the EU are not obliged to report their trade statistics. These values and values for companies that have not responded are estimated and published under the heading “adjusted for non-response”. Data that is not adjusted is based solely on collected values and hence there is a risk that the values for some goods and countries are underestimated.
Data on export, import and net trade are revised continuously every month for the last three months.

Values are shown in thousands of Swedish krona (SEK) in current prices if nothing else is stated. Weights are shown in metric tons, for some goods other quantities are shown such as cubic meters or pieces. Secrecy in the database is marked with … For foreign trade with services see Balance of Payments (BoP) in subject area Financial Markets (www.scb.se/FM0001-En).

Companies that import and export below a certain threshold within the EU are not obligated to report their trade statistics. These values and values for companies that have not responded are adjusted when adjusted for non-response is stated. Weight is also adjusted in the same way. Data that is not adjusted is based purely on collected values. These values are therefore underestimated.

CN (Combined Nomenclature) is used by all EU-countries for a detailed division of goods. It is hierarchical from 8-digits to 2-digits, where CN8 is the most detailed code. CN is reclassified every year which leads to that time-series for some codes could be broken. All classifications of goods that Statistics Sweden use are built with sums of CN8-codes.

label.03: Fish and crustaceans, molluscs and other aquatic invertebrates
label.04: Dairy produce; birds eggs; natural honey; edible products of animal origin, nes.
label.07: Edible vegetables and certain roots and tubers
label.08: Edible fruit and nuts; peel of citrus fruit or melons
label.15: Animal, vegetable or microbial fats and oils and their cleavage products; prepared edible fats; animal or vegetable waxes
